facts_disclosed: 4th pond plan scrapped — body tired, costs too high

turns.content: friend i am tired today. wake up 4am like always but body heavy, you know. getting old is not easy
Sorry to hear that. Feeling heavy and tired first thing in the morning is rough, especially when you're up that early. Has this been going on for a while or is it more of a recent thing?
more and more now. before i wake up 4am no problem, walk to pond, check everything, feel strong. now my knee hurt, my back hurt, you know. 63 year old body not like 40 year old body

That's a real shift to deal with. Your body is telling you something — the years of physical work add up, especially on knees and back. Working around ponds means a lot of bending, lifting, walking on uneven ground.

Are you able to rest during the day at all, or is it non-stop from 4am?
sometime i sit after lunch maybe 30 minute. but always something to do, you know. feed shrimp, check water, fix thing. 3 pond is a lot of work for one old man

3 ponds is a lot, even for someone younger. You're essentially running a non-stop operation by yourself with only some hired help for the heavy lifting.

Have you thought about scaling back at all, or getting more help? At some point, working through pain every day takes a toll not just on your body but on the quality of your work too.
you know i was thinking before about digging new pond. 4th pond on land behind my house. i think about it long time. but now — forget about it. scrapped. not happening
That sounds like a hard decision but an honest one. What made you finally decide to drop it?

two thing, friend. first my body. i cannot handle more work, you know. 3 pond already make me tired every day. add 4th pond? i break. second thing is money. cost go up to maybe 50,000,000 dong now to prepare land, dig pond, everything. before it was cheaper but now material price crazy

Both of those are solid reasons. 50 million dong is a huge investment, and if your body is already struggling with 3 ponds, taking on a 4th would be a serious risk to your health and to the quality of care for the ponds you already have.

Sometimes the smart business decision is knowing when not to expand. A farmer who runs 3 healthy, well-managed ponds will always do better than someone who stretches too thin across 4.

exactly. 3 pond is enough, you know. if i take care 3 pond good, yield good, profit good. if i try to do too much, all 4 pond suffer. like trying to carry too many bucket at one time — you drop all of them

That's a perfect way to put it. And the 50 million dong you're not spending on a new pond can go toward maintaining and improving what you already have — better equipment, repairs, maybe a little more hired help to take the pressure off your body.

Have you thought about getting more help with the daily work? Even one more person to share the early morning rounds could make a big difference for your knees and back.
maybe. my son he help sometime but he have his own work, you know. i think about it. for now 3 pond is the plan. no more, no less. i just need to take care of my body better

That's the right mindset. Taking care of yourself is taking care of the business — if you go down, the ponds go down with you.

For the knees and back, a few practical things that might help:
- Stretch for 5-10 minutes before you start the morning rounds
- If you're bending a lot to check trays, a long-handled net or scoop saves your back
- Knee compression sleeves — cheap, simple, and they help a lot on uneven ground
- Try to alternate heavy tasks with lighter ones throughout the day instead of doing all the physical work at once

Small adjustments can add years to how long you can keep doing this.
stretching, yes. my wife she always tell me to stretch but i never listen, you know. stubborn old man ha. okay friend. 3 pond is enough. that is good life. i go rest now before afternoon feeding

Listen to your wife — she's right about the stretching! And yes, rest before the afternoon round is smart.

You made a good decision about the 4th pond. No shame in it — that's wisdom, not giving up. Take care of yourself and those 3 ponds. Come back anytime.
